_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 8 verified claims. Key corrections: 'cancelled in 2018' → 'scrapped May 2018'; 'revived in 2023' → 're-approved March 2022' (the actual revival date); 'final approval received in 2025' specified as 'railway scheme approval 17 July 2025'; 'capped at RM45 billion' (overstates) → 'Budget 2023 target' (not a binding statutory cap); 'largest single rail project' (INCORRECT — ECRL is larger at RM74.96B) corrected. '51.6km' → '~51km orbital alignment'. TI-Malaysia attribution added for audit calls.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'cancelled in 2018' → 'scrapped May 2018' per Stage 3.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'revived in 2023' → 're-approved March 2022 with MRT Corp appointed' (the actual revival date).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'final approval received in 2025' specified to 'railway scheme approval 17 July 2025'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'capped at RM45 billion' framing softened to 'Budget 2023 target' / 'not a binding statutory cap'.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Malaysia's largest single rail project' replaced with 'ECRL is larger at RM74.96 billion total per MOT/MRL'. MRT3 retained as 'sits below ECRL but above other current rail builds'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — '51.6km elevated circle' → '~51 km orbital alignment' (round figure per MRT Corp).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Operational target 2032' qualified as 'Secondary reporting cites a 2032 operational target'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Every revival round adds review layers without binding caps' replaced with sourced revival-stages narrative.
ADDED (Stage 6 self-verified): TI-Malaysia 'Independent Expert Monitor and integrity pact before tender documents finalised' as audit-call attribution.

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): RM31B construction allocation; RM6-7B land acquisition; RM68B 2018 estimate; 2026 land-acquisition target; 2027 construction start; KV rail loop design; reduced headline does not eliminate cost-overrun risk.
